valuation_headline.method_why: Five-segment financial data conglomerate; each segment has distinct economics, moat depth, and appropriate discount rate. Segment-first SOTP with exit-multiple method per CONTRACT §2.2.

fair_value_today.basis: Daedalus SOTP: five segments at exit-multiple method, each at FY2028 normalization, discounted at segment WACC. Total segment EV $163.8B + corporate -$4.0B - net debt $11.84B = equity $148.0B / 296M shares = $500. The anchor — argued from segment moat quality and comparable multiples, not engineered toward price or consensus.

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 ($500) rolled forward at blended WACC 9.0% less dividend: $500 × 1.09 - $3.88 = $541; plus buyback accretion (share count 296M → 288M adds ~$6/share): $541 + $6 = ~$547, rounded to $545

detail.multiple_rationale: 12m target at 18.5x FY2026E EBITDA of $8.31B: EV = $153.7B; equity = $153.7B - $11.84B = $141.9B; 288M shares end-2026 = $492, rounded to $490. Rationale: (1) LEAN TOWARD BULL on narrative resolution — two consecutive quarters of SPGI Q1 2026 and Q2 2026 earnings showing no Market Intelligence subscription deterioration, plus the 'Is AI a Threat to S&P Global' Motley Fool piece (2026-06-20) arguing AI is a tailwind, suggest the narrative is softening. A modest multiple expansion from 17.6x to 18.5x is conservative (not the full re-rate to 21x); it represents one 'step' in the recovery; (2) LEAN AGAINST FULL CONVERGENCE in 12 months — the 'fair value' multiple of 20.8x requires the market to fully discount the AI narrative, which typically takes 2-3 earnings cycles of clean data to achieve; (3) Cross-check: at $490, SPGI trades at 22.1x NTM adjusted EPS ($22.23) — below the 27-28x MCO and well below the historical SPGI NTM P/E range of 27-33x. This is defensible and leaves upside from further re-rating. The gap between #3 ($545 full convergence) and #4 ($490 defensible) is $55/share — the re-rating premium the market has not yet awarded, which depends on 2-3 more clean earnings cycles. This is explicitly named as the 'narrative residual risk' in the conclusion.

street_reconstruction.what_price_implies: At $411 (17.6x TTM EV/EBITDA), the market prices in approximately: g=4.0% terminal growth, WACC=9.0%, ROIC=35% — implying ROIC compression of ~10pp from SPGI's demonstrated 45%+ levels. This is the AI-disruption-in-MI discount embedded in the price.
street_reconstruction.what_street_implies: Street median target $539 implies ~21-22x NTM EV/EBITDA. The street expects partial narrative correction within 12 months — consistent with our $490 target range.
street_reconstruction.history_anchor: SPGI 2021-2024 median EV/EBITDA approximately 22-25x; current 17.6x is ~25-30% below historical median. The last time SPGI traded at 17x was approximately 2015-2016 (pre-merger period when the company was smaller and less diversified).
street_reconstruction.our_justified_multiple: 20.8x (SOTP-derived; or Gordon at g=4.5%, r=9%, ROIC=40% → 19.7x, rounded to 20-21x)
street_reconstruction.agree_disagree: We agree with the street direction (bullish) but are more conservative on multiple: our $490 target implies 18.5x vs. street mean implying ~22x. Our fair value of $500 is below the street median of $539. We are 7% more conservative than consensus on 12m target. The bear scenario at $380 is 20% below current price and would require both AI disruption in MI AND issuance delay — we give this 20% probability.

conviction.label: MEDIUM
conviction.basis: HIGH conviction arguments: regulatory moats in Ratings/Indices are genuinely unassailable by AI (NRSRO franchise, benchmark network effect); stock at decade-low valuation; 21 analysts unanimously bullish; $6.2B annual capital returns are shareholder-accretive at current discount. MEDIUM-not-HIGH constraints: (1) the AI disruption narrative in MI is real and requires 2-3 more earnings cycles to definitively resolve — until then, the market may not award the full re-rate; (2) the Ratings issuance recovery depends on macro (Fed rate cuts) which are macro-dependent and uncertain as of June 2026; (3) net debt of $11.84B is non-trivial; if the market re-rates the equity DOWN on further AI news, the leverage amplifies the move. Conviction MEDIUM: the call is clear on value; the TIMING of re-rating is uncertain.
conviction.ensemble_stability: HIGH — three independent reads of this frozen snapshot would all produce BUY at $480-$510 fair value. The direction is not in question; the magnitude of the re-rate and the timing are.

bridge_logic: #1 fair value today $500.00 -> #3 fair value 12m $545.00 (× WACC 1.09 - div $3.88 + buyback accretion ~$6); #4 target 12m $490.00 (defensible 18.5x FY2026E EBITDA); implied return to #4 = +20.2%, to #3 = +32.6%, to street mean $532.80 = +29.7%. Rating: BUY. Conviction: MEDIUM.
